concerns.text: Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly state that the project state file (`state/projects/...yaml`) will be updated with content hashes upon execution. The plan mentions 'Content hashes... will be recorded' but lacks the specific mechanism (updating the state file) required by the Constitution to invalidate stale reviews.

Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly reference the 'Reference-Validator Agent' or the 'CITATION_TITLE_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD' mechanism required by the Constitution. The plan mentions 'Citations... will be validated' but does not confirm the specific automated gate defined in the Constitution.

Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly mention the 'Repository-Hygiene Agent' or the PII scan requirement. The plan mentions 'checksumming' and 'logging' but omits the specific automated PII scan gate mandated by the Constitution.

Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly state that 'Derived numbers MUST NOT be hand-typed into the paper' or that 'Every figure... MUST trace back to exactly one row in data/'. The plan mentions 'programmatic generation' but lacks the explicit prohibition on manual entry found in the Constitution.

Constitution Principle VII (Model Evaluation Transparency) is marked PASS, but the plan mentions 't-test' in the Constitution Check table (implied by 'permutation tests' in text, but Constitution VII explicitly requires 't-test against the null hypothesis'). The plan text says 'permutation tests', while Constitution VII says 't-test'. This is a methodological conflict in the plan's description of the Constitution compliance.

Constitution Principle VI (Materials Database Provenance) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly mention the requirement to 're-download, re-checksum, and update provenance metadata' for subsequent updates. The plan mentions 'source_metadata.yaml will be generated' but omits the update/re-download cycle required by the Constitution.
